pharma_classes.name: Controlled Room Temperature (CRT)
pharma_classes.description: Stable at room temperature. Most robust class, suitable for the majority of oral solid dosage forms.
pharma_classes.regulatory_basis: USP <1150>, Ph. Eur. Storage conditions, WHO Model Guidance

pharma_classes.name: Cool / Refrigerated (2-8 °C)
pharma_classes.description: Refrigerated storage required. Critical for most biologics and common vaccines.
pharma_classes.regulatory_basis: WHO TRS 961 Annex 9, EU GDP Guidelines 2013/C 343/01, USP <1079>

pharma_classes.name: Frozen (-20 to -15 °C)
pharma_classes.description: Frozen storage required. Very critical class for certain biologics and vaccines that lose potency above freezing.
pharma_classes.regulatory_basis: WHO TRS 961 Annex 9, USP <1079>, IATA TCR Chapter 17

pharma_classes.name: Ultra-Cold (-80 to -60 °C)
pharma_classes.description: Deep-frozen storage required. Extremely critical for mRNA vaccines and advanced therapy medicinal products.
pharma_classes.regulatory_basis: EU GDP Guidelines, WHO Immunization Supply Chain guidance, ISTA 7E
